sections.paragraph:
to be pure, Arab. id. نَقِىَ id.; Syriac to sprinkle water of purifying (prop. to purge), to offer a libation, to sacrifice, whence מְנַקִּית a sacrificial cup. In Kal only found, Jer 49:12 where נָקֹה inf. pleon. is joined to the conjugation Niphal.

Niphal נִקָּה-

(1) to be pure, metaph. innocent, followed by מִן to be void of any fault. Psa 19:14 Num 5:31 followed by מִן of person, Jud. 15:3 , נִקֵּיתִי מִפְּלִשְׁתִּים “I am free from blame towards the Philistines,” i.e. if I attack the Philistines it is not my fault, but their own.

(2) to be free from punishment, to be quit, Exo 21:19 Num 5:19 Pro 6:29, בָּהּ לֹא יִנָּקֶה כָּל־הַנֹּגֵעֲ “whoever toucheth her shall not be unpunished;” 11:21 .

(3) to be clear, free, from an oath, or obligation, Gen 24:8, 41 Gen 24:41.

(4) to be vacant, empty (spoken of a city), i.e. laid waste (like the Arab. Conj. X); Isa 3:26. Also used of men who are destroyed, extirpated, Zec 5:3.

Piel נִקָּה-

(1) to declare innocent, to absolve, Job 9:28 followed by מִן Psa 19:13 Job 10:14.

(2) to leave unpunished, to pardon, with an acc. of pers., Exo 20:7 1Ki 2:9 Jer 30:11 46:28 with an acc. of the crime. Joel 4:21 , וְנִקֵּתִי (better [but this is a conjecture]. נִקַּמְתִּי LXX. ἐκζητήσω) דָּמָם לֹא נִקֵּיתִי “I will avenge their blood, and will not leave it unavenged.” [But see Eng. Ver.] Absol. Exo 34:7, “forgiving iniquity, and transgression, and sin, וְנַקֵּה לֹא יְנַקֶּה but will not always pardon;” Num 14:18 Nah 1:3.

Derivatives, נָקִי, נָקִיא, נִקָּיֹון, מְנַקִּית.
